# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 7

This is turn 7. Turns 1 through 6 are consolidated in `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` or `current_manuscript.tex` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 7.

## Turn 7 — the global induction survives, after one uniform canonicality repair

There is first an attachment mismatch. Turn 6 records that Proposition 3.4 and all “insertion point” language were deleted, but the attached `current_manuscript.tex/.pdf` still contains the conditional version: the abstract calls §3.1 the unique insertion point, and §3.1 still states Proposition 3.4.    Thus the Turn 6 normalization patch must be reapplied to this source.

I then audited the remaining exact-image induction independently. Its recursion is structurally closed: the elementary \(M\)-stage subtracts only proper images with smaller marked kernel; the scalar \(R\)-pushouts reduce to determinant forms on the first nonscalar simple head; the completed-square identity moves every affine term into a scalar phase class
\[
\Delta_{\chi,\kappa}\in H^2(C,\mathbf F_2);
\]
and each corresponding phase cover again has strictly smaller marked kernel. Fourier inversion then reconstructs the full \(R\)-valued zero fibre. There is no circular appeal to equality of the source \(H^1\)-spaces.  

### The substantive gap: Lemma 6.3

The current proof of “Independence of normalized determinant data” says that “naturality of the split projections” kills the filtration-one ambiguity. That assertion is not proved. More importantly, the missing canonicality is later used when Proposition 6.15 declares that the norm-one group acts by isometries of the local base quadratic form.  

There is a uniform repair.

Let \(H=H_V\), let \(V\) be a nontrivial simple self-dual tame \(\mathbf F_2[H]\)-module, and let \(q\) be the prescribed invariant quadratic form. Suppose \(\kappa,\kappa'\in H^2(V\rtimes H,\mathbf F_2)\) both restrict to \(q\) on \(V\) and both restrict trivially to the zero section \(H\hookrightarrow V\rtimes H\). Set
\[
\xi=\kappa-\kappa'.
\]
Then \(\operatorname{res}_V(\xi)=0\). The seven-term sequence for
\[
1\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow V\rtimes H\longrightarrow H\longrightarrow1
\]
contains
\[
H^2(H,\mathbf F_2)
 \xrightarrow{\inf}
\ker(\operatorname{res}_V)
 \xrightarrow{\mathrm{edge}}
H^1(H,V^\vee).
\]

Here
\[
H^1(H,V^\vee)=0.
\]
Indeed, in the unramified case \(H\) has odd order, so positive-degree cohomology vanishes. In the ramified case, the normal odd inertia subgroup has no fixed vector on \(V^\vee\); Hochschild–Serre therefore makes all \(H\)-cohomology of \(V^\vee\) vanish. Equivalently, one may invoke the manuscript’s projectivity lemma. 

Consequently \(\xi=\inf(\delta)\) for some \(\delta\in H^2(H,\mathbf F_2)\). Pullback along the zero section is a left inverse to inflation, while both normalized classes vanish there. Hence
\[
\delta=0,\qquad \xi=0.
\]

Thus:

\[
\boxed{\text{The zero-section-normalized base class }\kappa_q^0
\text{ is unique in cohomology.}}
\]

This proves independence of the factor set, the correction functions, and the chosen split regular embedding. Normalized cocycle representatives then differ by a normalized coboundary.

It also supplies exactly the functoriality needed later. If
\[
u\in\operatorname{Aut}_H(V,q),
\]
then \((v,h)\mapsto(uv,h)\) fixes the zero section and preserves the restriction \(q\), so uniqueness gives
\[
u^*[\kappa_q^0]=[\kappa_q^0].
\]
Therefore
\[
Q_{\mathrm{loc}}^0(u_*x)=Q_{\mathrm{loc}}^0(x).
\]

In the unramified branch of Proposition 6.15, an element \(u\) of the norm-one group is \(H\)-linear and preserves the polar form. The difference
\[
x\longmapsto q(ux)+q(x)
\]
is consequently an \(H\)-invariant linear form, hence zero because
\((V^\vee)^H=0\). Thus \(u\) is a \(q\)-isometry, and the asserted norm-one invariance of \(Q_{\mathrm{loc}}^0\) now follows rigorously. The Hermitian-line classification and the minus-type zero count are therefore justified.

### One smaller correction in the \(R\)-stage

Lemma 7.2 proves only that \(R\) is central in \(K\), not necessarily in \(Y\). Accordingly, the proof of Lemma 9.3 should not begin “the kernel \(R\) is central.” The correct statement is:

\[
R\text{ is elementary abelian and }K\text{ centralizes }R,
\]
so the \(Y\)-action on \(R\) factors through \(C=Y/K\). Duality therefore identifies
\[
H^2_{\Gamma,\rho}(R)^\vee\simeq(R^\vee)^C.
\]
For \(\lambda\in(R^\vee)^C\), the quotient \(R/\ker\lambda\) has trivial \(C\)-action, and hence its scalar pushout is a central double cover. Every subsequent Fourier and cover-transform argument is unchanged.

The candidate wild relation therefore remains unchanged. The necessary actions are to restore the Turn 6 marked-normalization replacement in the attached source, replace the proof of Lemma 6.3 by the uniqueness argument above, and correct the centrality wording in Lemma 9.3.

ESTABLISHED — The exact-image/Fourier induction is structurally well-founded; the missing determinant-class canonicality is repaired uniformly by the seven-term sequence and \(H^1(H_V,V^\vee)=0\).

GAP — The attached manuscript is the stale conditional version and does not yet contain either the Turn 6 normalization patch or the two Turn 7 corrections.

NEXT — Reapply the normalization integration, insert the uniqueness/isometry corollary after Lemma 6.3, correct the \(R\)-module wording, then compile and rerun the obsolete-label/dependency sweep.

[AUDITED-REPAIR]
